chatgpt.headline: It can draft. You decide whether the draft is right.
chatgpt.body: ChatGPT will produce a plausible paragraph on almost anything. What it cannot do is tell when the plausible paragraph is wrong for this room, this moment, this person. That part still belongs to you.

github-copilot.headline: It writes the code. You make the call that the code shouldn't exist yet.
github-copilot.body: Copilot is excellent at filling in the obvious next line. The decision about whether the line, the function, or the entire system is the right thing to be building — that's still a judgement call no autocomplete can make.

midjourney.headline: It can generate ten thousand variations. It can't tell you which one matters.
midjourney.body: Midjourney will give you a dazzling image in seconds. Whether the image carries what you needed it to carry — that requires the eye you spent years building.

claude.headline: It will sound thoughtful. You decide whether it's actually correct.
claude.body: Claude is calibrated to sound reasonable, which is dangerous when the reasoning is wrong. The work of catching a confident error before it ships is still a human function.

perplexity.headline: It summarises the search. It can't tell you what to ask.
perplexity.body: Perplexity is fast at synthesizing sources. The harder skill — knowing what question is actually worth asking, what counts as evidence, when to push past the easy answer — remains yours.

notion-ai.headline: It can structure the doc. You decide what's worth being in the doc.
notion-ai.body: Notion AI is a competent first-pass organizer. What it can't do is read the room and know which things are politically OK to write down, and which ones aren't.

alphafold.headline: It predicts the structure. You decide what to ask next.
alphafold.body: AlphaFold reshaped what's tractable in structural biology. The taste required to know which prediction is actually meaningful — which protein matters, why, for whom — is still a scientist's job.

elevenlabs.headline: It produces the voice. It can't produce the meaning behind it.
elevenlabs.body: ElevenLabs can clone a voice to uncanny accuracy. What that voice is saying, and whether saying it serves the person on the other end, is a human responsibility the model cannot inherit.

cursor.headline: It writes the patch. You decide whether the patch belongs in the system at all.
cursor.body: Cursor is a remarkable pair-programmer. The harder craft — designing what the system is actually trying to do, refusing the wrong feature, holding the architecture in your head — is still yours.

gemini.headline: It will answer most questions confidently. You decide which confident answers to trust.
gemini.body: Gemini is fluent across modalities. Fluency at this scale tempts deference, but the work of double-checking, sense-checking, and choosing which output to act on remains the human signal in the loop.

adobe-firefly.headline: It can generate the asset. It can't make the brand decision behind it.
adobe-firefly.body: Firefly produces commercially-safe imagery quickly. What it cannot do is decide what the brand should look like, what tone is right for this campaign, what the audience will actually feel — those calls live in the room you're in.

runway-ml.headline: It can generate the clip. You make the cut that means something.
runway-ml.body: Runway is extraordinary at producing footage. Editing — the human decision of what to leave in, what to leave on the floor, what beat carries the story — is still where the work lives.

suno.headline: It can compose. It can't tell you why the song needs to exist.
suno.body: Suno will write a credible song from a prompt. The reason for the song, the artist behind it, the listener it's reaching for — those are the questions music asks, and they're still yours to answer.

harvey-ai.headline: It can draft the memo. You take the position the firm will stand behind.
harvey-ai.body: Harvey is exceptional at legal drafting. The judgement about which interpretation is right for this client, this jurisdiction, this risk appetite — that's still the work that requires a person whose name is on the file.

khanmigo.headline: It can tutor. It can't see when a student has given up.
khanmigo.body: Khanmigo will patiently walk a learner through any topic. What it can't do is notice the quiet kid who stopped trying two weeks ago, and ask them what's going on at home. That's still a teacher's job.

openevidence.headline: It surfaces the evidence. You decide what to do for this patient.
openevidence.body: OpenEvidence is a fast way to land on the relevant clinical literature. The harder skill — translating the literature into a decision for the specific person sitting in front of you — is still medicine.

gong.headline: It can transcribe the call. It can't tell you what the customer actually meant.
gong.body: Gong is excellent at capturing sales conversations. What it cannot capture is the hesitation in the buyer's voice when they say yes, or the political situation on their team that means yes today is no next month.

apollo-io.headline: It can find the prospect. You decide whether they should be reached at all.
apollo-io.body: Apollo is a powerful list-building engine. The integrity layer — whether this outreach is welcome, useful, or just noise added to someone's day — is a call no targeting algorithm can make for you.

jasper.headline: It can fill the calendar. You make the work worth reading.
jasper.body: Jasper will produce on-brand marketing copy at scale. What it cannot do is take the small creative risk that turns a campaign from filler into something a reader actually remembers.

notebooklm.headline: It can summarise the corpus. You decide what's worth taking from it.
notebooklm.body: NotebookLM is a strong synthesizer of long documents. The harder move — picking the one idea inside a hundred pages that's actually load-bearing for your work — is still yours.

replit-agent.headline: It can stand up the project. You decide whether the project is worth standing up.
replit-agent.body: Replit Agent will spin up a working app from a sentence. Whether the app is solving a real problem, or just impressing a demo audience — that's the question no agent answers for you.

bolt-new.headline: It can ship a prototype before lunch. You decide whether to ship it at all.
bolt-new.body: Bolt.new collapses the time-to-first-screen for any web idea. The risk: shipping the first thing you saw work, because the cost of trying again feels too high once it exists.

lovable.headline: It builds the SaaS shell. You build the reason anyone would use it.
lovable.body: Lovable produces convincing app scaffolds. What it cannot conjure is the founder's reason — the specific human you're trying to help, and the small original move that makes the product worth opening.

windsurf.headline: It pairs with you fluently. You decide what's worth pairing on.
windsurf.body: Windsurf is one of the smoothest in-editor agents. The selection layer — what to refactor, what to leave alone, which complaint from a teammate is actually load-bearing — is still yours.

aider.headline: It edits the repo. You hold the long thread of what the repo is becoming.
aider.body: Aider is a wonderful terminal-native coder. What it can't hold across sessions is the through-line of architectural intent — what this codebase is for, six months from now, when nobody remembers the original conversation.

gnome-deepmind.headline: It proposes new materials. You decide which ones are worth synthesising.
gnome-deepmind.body: GNoME generates millions of stable crystal candidates. The taste that matters now is the materials scientist's — which proposal opens a door, which one is a clever dead end, which one is worth the lab time.

atomwise.headline: It finds the hit. You decide whether the hit is worth pursuing.
atomwise.body: Atomwise screens compounds at scale most pharma teams can't match by hand. The translational call — does this hit address a real unmet need, will the patients be findable — remains a human-led judgement.

alphasense.headline: It surfaces the signal. You decide what the signal means for your portfolio.
alphasense.body: AlphaSense pulls the relevant excerpt from a thousand earnings calls. The interpretive layer — what this implies for your conviction, your sizing, your timing — is still where edge lives.

deepseek.headline: It's fast and cheap. You decide whether fast and cheap is what the question needs.
deepseek.body: DeepSeek has remarkable performance at a fraction of frontier cost. The question of whether to use it — whether speed is what matters here, or whether the problem deserves a more careful model — is still yours.

consensus.headline: It finds the studies. You decide which studies you actually trust.
consensus.body: Consensus is excellent at surfacing scientific literature relevant to a question. The harder skill — sample size, methodology, conflicts, replication — is the part you still have to read for yourself.

gamma.headline: It can ship the deck. You decide what's worth saying in front of people.
gamma.body: Gamma turns notes into polished slides in minutes. The narrative spine — what's the one thing the audience needs to leave the room believing — is still the work that doesn't auto-generate.

tome.headline: It can structure the pitch. You decide whether the pitch is true.
tome.body: Tome is great at scaffolding a presentation. Whether the company behind the pitch actually does what the slides promise — that's a story you still have to tell yourself first, honestly.

descript.headline: It edits the recording. You decide what shouldn't have been said.
descript.body: Descript makes audio editing fast enough to be dangerous. The editorial call — what stays, what gets cut for clarity, what gets cut for kindness — is still a human responsibility.

otter-ai.headline: It captures every word. You decide which words mattered.
otter-ai.body: Otter is a faithful transcriber of meetings. The summary that's actually useful — what was decided, what got dodged, who's accountable for what — is still the work after the meeting, not in it.

fathom.headline: It logs the call. You decide what the customer is really saying.
fathom.body: Fathom captures every sales conversation with timestamps and tags. The deeper read — what the buyer's hesitation means, whether the budget signal is real — still requires you in the room.

n8n.headline: It automates the workflow. You decide whether the workflow is worth keeping.
n8n.body: n8n is a powerful self-hosted automation builder. The harder question — is this workflow solving a real problem, or am I automating a habit that should've been deleted — is yours alone.

zapier.headline: It glues the tools together. You decide which tools deserve to be glued.
zapier.body: Zapier turns 'I need this to talk to that' into a five-minute fix. The discipline is choosing not to wire up every possible connection — most automations end up creating more noise than they remove.

runway.headline: It can make the cut faster. It can't tell you what the cut is for.
runway.body: Runway is changing video production at the timeline level. The directorial question — what story you're telling, who you're telling it for, why this beat lands — is still where editing actually happens.

leonardo-ai.headline: It generates the asset. You decide whether the asset is right for the brand.
leonardo-ai.body: Leonardo is a strong generative imagery tool. The brand call — does this match the visual language we're building, would a real designer have made this choice — still requires a person with skin in the brand.

sudowrite.headline: It can finish the sentence. You decide whether the sentence should exist.
sudowrite.body: Sudowrite is a real friend to drafting. The judgment about which sentences earn their place, and which ones are just keeping the word count moving, is still the writer's lonely call.
